name: Collection Log
description: An account- or profile-level record of discovered items, characters, enemies, lore entries, cosmetics, achievements, or other long-term collectibles.
design_notes: A collection log gives players long-term goals outside a single session. The design should define which discoveries are automatic, which require claim actions, which are hidden, how retroactive unlocks work after content updates, and how privacy settings affect shared profile pages.
design_purpose: Give players a persistent sense of completion and discovery across sessions.
Expose optional goals without forcing them into the core progression path.
Support account migration, cross-save records, and retrospective unlock checks.
player_fantasy: The player feels their account remembers what they have found, mastered, and completed over the life of the game.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Account owner for collection records, platform sync, and privacy settings.

parameters.name: profile_id
parameters.typical_range: local profile or save slot identifier
parameters.description: Profile segment used when one account has multiple save slots.

parameters.name: collection_entry_id
parameters.description: Stable content key for the discovered or claimed entry.

parameters.name: unlock_timestamp_ms
parameters.description: Recorded unlock moment used for sorting, audit, and sync conflict resolution.

parameters.name: discovery_source_id
parameters.description: Source event that granted the entry and helps debug retroactive unlocks.

parameters.name: retroactive_unlock_policy
parameters.description: Rule for awarding entries added after players already met requirements.

parameters.name: privacy_visibility_id
parameters.description: Sharing rule for profile pages, social features, and platform presence.

edge_cases: A content update renames or removes collection_entry_id values already present in old saves.
The same discovery is earned offline on two devices before cross-save conflict resolution.
A hidden spoiler entry is discovered but should not reveal locked neighboring entries.
A migrated save has proof of completion but lacks the original discovery source event.
Platform privacy settings forbid publishing entries that the local UI marks public.
common_bugs: Collection entries are unlocked from UI preview instead of the committed gameplay event.
Cross-save merge picks the newest profile and loses older collection entries.
Retroactive scans run every launch and repeatedly show unlock notifications.
Display names are used as keys and break when localization changes.
Hidden entries count toward completion percentages before they are revealed to the player.
balancing_notes: Collection logs should make optional mastery visible without making normal play feel incomplete.
Hidden entries are strongest when they protect surprise, not when they obscure basic progress.
Completion rewards should respect spoiler pacing and avoid requiring unreasonable random drops.
accessibility_notes: Provide search, filters, progress text, and clear locked-state descriptions for large logs.
Avoid communicating rarity, category, or completion only through color.
Let players hide spoiler entries or reduce notification frequency.

implementation_notes.unity: Store collection_entry_id records in an account profile service, not scene objects. Use ScriptableObject or addressable definitions for entry metadata, commit unlocks through one account transaction, and sync platform achievements only after the local entry is persisted.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep collection definitions as Resources and profile records in a versioned save dictionary keyed by collection_entry_id. Run retroactive scans behind a migration marker so old saves are fixed once without repeating notifications.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Data Assets for collection definitions and SaveGame or online profile state for unlock records. Platform service calls should be idempotent and respect privacy_visibility_id before publishing profile progress.
implementation_notes.web: Persist collection state in account storage or IndexedDB with content version and unlock timestamps. When cross-save is available, merge by stable entry ids and keep discovery_source_id audit data for conflict resolution.

example_games.title: Vampire Survivors
example_games.note: Uses collection and unlock records as long-term account goals.

example_games.title: Hades
example_games.note: Tracks discovered boons, keepsakes, and other profile-level records.
